REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS RONNIE PILGRIM, PEARSON PROPERTIES, INC., GRETCHEN PEARSON, JEFFREY MEARS, CATHERINE CROSS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF SHALL NOT INTERCHANGEABLY USE "DEFENDANT" AND "DEFENDANTS" AND MUST SPECIFY EXACTLY WHO DID WHAT, WHEN, AND IN WHAT MANNER. AS TO THE CONTRACT BASED CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACT EITHER (A) SHOWING THAT THERE IS A CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND MOVING DEFENDANTS, I.E. THAT PLAINTIFF IS A PARTY TO TDS (TDA) OR STATEWIDE ADVISORY, OR (B) THAT BY VIRTUE OF EXECUTING TDS AND ADVISORY MOVING DEFENDANTS BECAME PARTIES TO THE PURCHASE AGREEMENT, IF PLAINTIFF HAS LEGAL BASIS FOR SUCH ALLEGATION. Defendant Lee Scott And Hj Home Investments, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Lee Scott And HJ Home Investments, Inc.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is
Sustained with leave to amend as to HJ Home Investment Inc. ("HJ") as to causes of action 2, 3, 4, and 9. The Toribios have leave to amend to allege facts showing that despite their discussion with Ms. Lerhman on May 1, 2014, they were on inquiry notice and could have reasonably only discovered the damage on June 30, 2014 when they reviewed the MG Report. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808-809.) Plaintiffs also have leave to plead the conspiracy allegations as general charging allegations rather than a distinct cause of action. (See Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd., (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-511.) Mr. Scott's demurrer to the fraud claims (causes of action 2, 3, 4, and 9) are sustained without leave to amend. Paragraph 50 of the amended complaint indicates that plaintiffs learned of the fraud/concealment on June 30, 2014 when they received the MG Report. Plaintiffs added Mr. Scott as a defendant on July 31, 2017, more than 3 years later. The statute of limitations for the fraud claims is 3-years per CCP 338. The relation back doctrine does not apply because plaintiffs did not substitute Mr. Scott into the action as a fictitious Doe defendant pursuant to CCP 474. (See Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 151.) Mr. Scott's demurrer to causes of action 1, 5, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs admit that they do not know whether Mr. Scott was a broker at paragraph 4 of the amended complaint. The court grants judicial notice of the grant deed and that document shows that HJ was the sole seller/transferor. Based on this information, plaintiffs cannot in good faith plead causes of action 1, 5, and 6. HJ and Mr. Scott's argument about whether or not they negotiated with Ms. Lehrman fails to dispose of the claims. The key fact is that defendants knew of the MG Report and suppressed it to the Toribios detriment. (Amended Complaint, par. 42.) Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
